highlights.item: Homepage seed content has been reworked toward a real exterior-cleaning marketing site instead of the template fallback.
Instant quote UX is live on the homepage and posts usable lead data into Payload form submissions.
Customer-facing privacy, terms, refund, and contact-SLA pages are now seeded as Payload `pages` content and intended to be linked from the public footer/contact flow.
Launch polish is now actively tracking the shared public/admin visual system, including theme tokens, typography, and QA for login/admin shells.

The homepage renderer now drops placeholder seed media on `home` and uses masked section shells so service, pricing, and CTA content read like intentional marketing panels instead of template leftovers.

The live homepage now uses a compact code-viewer style service surface with icon-led tabs, inline `Start estimate` actions in the file rail, and a side-by-side detail pane instead of stacked service/pricing explainer grids.

The seeded public-site narrative now includes a founder-story `about` page plus a homepage panel that explains the business as two young entrepreneurs building serious operating experience ahead of college.
Phase 03 now explicitly includes a media-led marketing refresh so homepage, about, and contact surfaces can use Payload-managed imagery and service proof instead of staying mostly text-only.

The homepage now renders the seeded Payload layout blocks directly instead of relying on hardcoded `HomeMarketingPanels`, and the reusable `serviceGrid` block now supports media, highlights, and pricing cues for image-led service cards.
Seed media now includes exterior-cleaning placeholder imagery with captions, the about page uses a stronger visual hero, and the shared header/footer no longer show the template Payload logo.

A new queued UX refinement is now tracked for `/contact`: stronger contrast, less copy, clearer action hierarchy, logo presence in the request card, heavier icon usage, and compact tabs with one detail panel for dense support text.

next.item: Replace the current placeholder property photos with generated or real Grime Time imagery for driveway action, house washing, and commercial surfaces.
Add a quick-edit admin path for reviews, testimonials, and before/after proof so the refreshed public visuals have matching trust content.
Decide whether `/contact` should stay a bespoke route or be brought fully under the Payload page-layout model like the homepage and about page.
Execute the `/contact` UX refresh using a compact tab-detail pattern and remove faded/low-emphasis presentation that currently obscures the form action.
Complete launch QA across public routes, login, and admin screens after reseed.
